title: lonchocarpus retifer
intro: Lonchocarpus retifer is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the "monkey tree" due to its unique fruit shape resembling monkey faces. Traditionally, Lonchocarpus retifer has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of Lonchocarpus retifer are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to have curative effects when consumed.
